text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between rolling hills and a sparkling river, there lived a kind-hearted old man named Wei. Wei was known throughout the village for his wisdom and the beautiful stories he told around the bonfire each night.

One autumn evening, as the leaves painted the ground in a mosaic of reds and golds, a young girl named Mei wandered into the village. She had lost her way while picking wildflowers and found herself at the edge of the village, on the outskirts where the path to her home seemed to end. Hungry and scared, she sat down under a large oak tree and wept.

Wei saw her from his window and came out to see what troubled her. He asked her about her name and why she was crying. Mei explained that she was lost and that her family would be worried sick about her. Wei listened patiently and then invited her to join him by the bonfire.

As they sat by the fire, Wei began to tell her
